title: Kente Cloth and the Architecture of Akan Identity
creator: Asante Weavers, Bonwire
summary: Kente is not merely decorative cloth. Each named pattern encodes cosmology, genealogy, and social ethics.
abstract: Kente functions as a textile archive of Akan political memory and moral philosophy.
description: This entry treats Kente as both material culture and epistemic infrastructure.
The archive value lies not only in surviving textiles but in the vocabularies and relationships that keep those meanings legible.
notes: Community custodianship takes priority over extractive image capture or reproduction.

title: The Wretched of the Earth
creator: Frantz Fanon
summary: A foundational anti-colonial text analysing colonial violence, political subject formation, and liberation.
abstract: A foundational anti-colonial text analysing colonial violence, political subject formation, and the difficult psychic work of national liberation.
description: This record centres Fanon's argument that colonialism reorganises both institutions and interior life.
Within the archive, the text operates as a major theoretical hinge linking liberation movements in Africa to broader decolonial discourse across the Global South and diaspora.
notes: Different editions and translations vary in apparatus, forewords, and pagination.

title: Decolonising Design in Africa
creator: Yaw Ofosu-Asare
summary: Maps the epistemological stakes of design education across the African continent through Sankofa methodology.
abstract: A contemporary study of decolonial design education across Africa, using Sankofa as both analytic lens and design method.
description: This record documents a recent intervention in design studies that repositions African knowledge systems as generative rather than supplementary.
The text is useful as both research source and programmatic framework.
notes: Useful companion record for Sankofa-focused entries and related design pedagogy material.
